_note: Batch d5+d6 (round 2, medieval 800-1400 AD): 32 of 33 round-1 parent ids given documented parents (and, where available, full/notable siblings) going one generation further back. SKIPPED 1: SPH_JEAN_ANGOULEME (Jean of Orléans, Count of Angoulême, 1399-1467) — his parents (Louis I, Duke of Orleans & Valentina Visconti) are ALREADY fully documented under a DUPLICATE round-1 id, SPJ_JOHNANGOULEME, in 40_spouseparents_u4.json (same family: Louis I x Valentina Visconti -> CHARLESORLEANS, SPJ_JOHNANGOULEME). No new family created to avoid fabricating a second parent set for the same historical person; flagging this pre-existing round-1 duplicate (SPH_JEAN_ANGOULEME vs SPJ_JOHNANGOULEME) for future cleanup/merge. High-value cross-links made to the EXISTING tree in this batch: Richard FitzAlan x Eleanor of Lancaster (existing SPJ_ELEANORLANCASTER) branches into both the Joan FitzAlan and (via new daughter Alice FitzAlan) the Margaret/Eleanor Holland families; John II of France x Bonne of Luxembourg (existing JOHNIIFR x BONNELUX, whose son Charles V/CHARLESVFR is already in the tree) gains two new daughters, Joan of Valois (mother of Charles III of Navarre) and Isabelle of Valois (mother of Valentina Visconti); Charles V x Joanna of Bourbon (existing CHARLESVFR x JOANNABOURBON) gains son Louis I of Orleans; Alfonso XI (existing) gains an illegitimate union with Leonor de Guzmán producing Henry II of Castile, whose own marriage to Juana Manuel in turn links to the existing, previously-parentless John I of Castile (JOHNICAST); Edmund of Langley (existing EOL, previously childless in the tree) gains three children incl. Richard of Conisburgh; John I of Portugal (existing JOAOI) gains illegitimate son Afonso I of Braganza; John the Fearless x Margaret of Bavaria (existing round-1 couple) gains daughter Marie of Burgundy. New nodes use the R2H_ prefix. Sourced to Wikipedia and, for the Castilian Padilla/Hinestrosa and Scottish Drummond/Montifex lines, to specialist genealogical references (Real Academia de la Historia, Fundación Casa Ducal de Medinaceli, History of Parliament Online, thepeerage.com) — flagged moderate-confidence where sources vary. Verified 2026-07-17.
